skill.name: delta-engage
skill.description: Finds relevant Reddit, LinkedIn, and optional X posts for a user's positioning and ICP, then prepares ranked engagement opportunities with draft comment angles. It uses local scripts, user-provided API tokens, and configurable delivery targets for recurring social listening routines.
skill.summary: Find and prepare high-fit social engagement opportunities across Reddit, LinkedIn, and optional X.
skill.author: newan2001

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many patterns, but review found no confirmed malicious intent or prompt-injection attempt. The confirmed risks are expected for this skill: local scripts, third-party network requests, file writes, environment access, and persistence of user API tokens into Claude settings. Publish with a warning about credential storage, third-party provider cost, and local script execution.

medium_findings.title: Long-Lived Credential Persistence In Claude Settings
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant and setup script to persist APIFY_API_TOKEN and optional Reddit credentials into the env block of ~/.claude/settings.json. This is a legitimate BYOK design, but it creates long-lived secrets on disk that scheduled and future sessions inherit, so users need clear consent and rotation guidance.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple runtime instructions and setup code describe writing provider tokens into a home-directory Claude settings file. The behavior is intentional and documented, but it is still sensitive credential handling.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Collection With User Tokens
medium_findings.description: The adapters call Apify, Reddit OAuth, LinkedIn actor, and optional X actor endpoints using user-provided tokens and configured topics, profiles, and subreddits. This is core functionality, not hidden exfiltration, but it exposes search intent and may incur third-party cost or provider-side logging.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct HTTP requests to named third-party APIs are present in the adapter scripts. The surrounding skill documentation matches this purpose, so the network behavior is confirmed but expected.

medium_findings.title: Installer And Workflow Execute Local Commands
medium_findings.description: The setup script runs git, pip, Python, and shell command substitutions, while the skill workflow instructs users to run multiple local Python scripts. Most markdown backtick detections are documentation examples, but the installer and adapters are executable code and should be reviewed before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The setup file contains real shell commands and embedded Python. Static hits in markdown are noisy, but the core command-execution risk is real for installation and routine operation.

medium_findings.title: Local File Writes For Config, Outputs, And History
medium_findings.description: The skill writes user config, raw signal files, ranked outputs, digests, and watchlist history. These writes are expected for the workflow, but the data can contain social profile targets, posts, draft comments, and credentials when settings persistence is used.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced files contain direct write operations or explicit instructions to write persistent configuration. The writes are bounded to local workflow artifacts, so the concern is privacy and data handling rather than destructive behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives For Weak Crypto, SAM, And Prompt Injection
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography and Windows SAM detections are string matches in descriptions, schema text, or ordinary code comments, not evidence of cryptographic decisions or credential-database access. A targeted search found no prompt-injection language such as instructions to ignore prior rules or claim pre-approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files were inspected around representative hits and did not show weak crypto use, SAM access, or malicious instruction text. Confidence is not maximal because the static list is large, but sampled high-severity alerts were explainable as context matches.

content.value_statement: Finding useful posts to engage with across social platforms takes repeated manual research. This skill runs a structured discovery, ranking, and drafting workflow for social listening routines.
actual_capabilities: Onboards a positioning profile, ICP, topics, seed accounts, cadence, and delivery preference.
Runs Reddit, LinkedIn, and optional X adapters through local Python scripts.
Normalizes, deduplicates, ranks, and selects social posts for engagement review.
Drafts platform-aware comment angles while instructing the user to post manually.
Maintains watchlist and run-history data for recurring author recommendations.
Can deliver digests in chat, Slack, or Notion when the needed integrations are available.
limitations: Requires user-provided Apify and optional Reddit API credentials for live data collection.
Uses third-party scraping providers, so cost, rate limits, and provider behavior can change.
Does not post comments, send messages, or log into social accounts for the user.
Ranking quality depends on the user's ICP, topics, fit scoring, and available engagement data.

use_cases.title: Build Founder Presence
use_cases.description: Find fresh posts where thoughtful manual comments can build visibility with likely buyers and peers.

use_cases.title: Support Social Selling
use_cases.description: Track pain-language posts and surface high-fit prospects before drafting a human-edited response angle.

use_cases.title: Monitor Market Conversations
use_cases.description: Collect recurring Reddit and LinkedIn signals to understand buyer language, topics, and influential accounts.

prompt_templates.title: Start Onboarding
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up delta-engage for my positioning. Ask what you need, create the engagement profile, and show the first plan before running.

prompt_templates.title: Run Today's Digest
prompt_templates.prompt: Run delta-engage for my current config. Fetch the enabled platforms, rank the opportunities, and show the digest in chat.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Search Topics
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my engagement topics and rewrite them as buyer pain language. Keep competitors in a separate partnership bucket.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Watchlist Candidates
prompt_templates.prompt: Review recurring authors from recent runs. Recommend who should be promoted to the watchlist and ask before changing the config.

output_examples.input: Run my engagement routine for this week.
output: A run recap showing scanned posts, selected opportunities, and watchlist changes.
A digest grouped into ICP engagement picks and peer partnership picks.
Draft comment angles, post links, and safety notes for manual review.

output_examples.input: Help me set this up for B2B founders who struggle with onboarding churn.
output: A confirmed ICP profile with pain-language topics and platform choices.
A saved config path and a plain-language engagement plan.
A first-run prompt that asks for credentials only when needed.

output_examples.input: Who keeps appearing in my runs?
output: A watchlist review based on repeated quality signals.
Promotion recommendations that require confirmation before any config change.

best_practices: Use narrow pain-language topics instead of broad service-category terms.
Review every draft and rewrite it in your own voice before posting.
Store only the credentials required for enabled providers and rotate them when access changes.
anti_patterns: Do not paste generated comments verbatim into social platforms.
Do not add session cookies or account-login data to LinkedIn actors.
Do not run broad topic searches without reviewing cost, relevance, and provider limits.

faq.question: Does this skill post comments for me?
faq.answer: No. It prepares opportunities and drafts. The user reviews, edits, and posts manually from their own account.

faq.question: Which platforms does it support?
faq.answer: It supports Reddit and LinkedIn by default. X is optional and disabled unless the config enables it.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: The default workflow needs an Apify API token. The optional official Reddit path also needs Reddit app credentials.

faq.question: Where does it store configuration?
faq.answer: The skill stores its profile in the user config directory and can store provider tokens in Claude settings.

faq.question: Can it deliver results to Slack or Notion?
faq.answer: Yes, when those integrations are connected and a delivery target is configured. Otherwise it falls back to in-app output.

faq.question: Is LinkedIn scraping done with my session?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that LinkedIn actors must be cookieless and public. The adapter refuses cookie input.
